explanation: or more likely a patio umbrella

discriminators: The DARK, DEAD extreme of the RAIN material-impedance axis. Cloth is the highest-loss surface in the set: a patio umbrella, awning or tarp absorbs the droplet almost completely. Lowest spectral_centroid_hz (~1.6 kHz), steepest spectral_slope_db_per_octave (-10.5 dB/oct), lowest high_band_energy, and the shortest decay_time_seconds_60db (~10 ms) of any RAIN surface.
But a taut fabric membrane still THUMPS: individual drops are resolvable low-mid thuds, so crest_factor stays well above RAIN-VEGETATION's diffuse hiss and low_band_energy is the highest in RAIN.
Against RAIN-METAL: the exact opposite end of the axis — a factor of ~4 in centroid and ~9 in ring time. If the two ever score close, the measurement is wrong.
Against RAIN-VEGETATION: comparably damped, comparably dark; the separator is transient structure (cloth thumps, foliage hisses) and low_band_energy.
Against RAIN-PLASTIC: a tarp is the boundary case. Plastic is stiffer and brighter with an audible tick; cloth has none.

explanation: Rain on asphalt or concrete or patio tile. Rain on a hard slappy solid surface.

discriminators: UCS, verbatim: 'a hard slappy solid surface'. Concrete and asphalt are a rigid, massive half-space — the droplet's energy radiates as a broadband SLAP with essentially no resonance. Bright-ish (centroid ~3.8 kHz) but DEAD: decay_time_seconds_60db ~20 ms, four times shorter than RAIN-METAL.
Slappy means discrete: individual droplet impacts stay resolvable, so crest_factor is the highest of the hard surfaces and stationarity is lower than the diffuse surfaces.
Against RAIN-METAL: same brightness family, opposite ring. Bright + ringing = metal; bright + dead = concrete. decay_time_seconds_60db is the axis.
Against RAIN-WOOD: wood is a lossy plate — noticeably darker (~2.6 kHz) with a mid-band drum body.
Against RAIN-WATER: puddles are darker and produce pitched droplet plops (measurable harmonicity), not slaps.

provenance.reason: the reasoned gate rejected 85% of this subcategory's own true clips
the reasoned gate rejected 36% of this subcategory's own true clips

discriminators: 'Rain on mixed materials' — by definition the centroid of the whole material-impedance axis, with every prior set to the middle of the RAIN family and wide deviations so it never beats a committed surface on that surface's own evidence.
This is the RAIN abstention target: when the damping/brightness measurement lands between two surfaces, or when no surface word appears in the filename, GENERAL is the correct answer.
A general downpour still gates as rain: a dense stochastic texture, long, unpitched, with a high onset_rate_per_second. It cannot be a single impact.
If a specific surface word (metal, tin, leaves, umbrella, pavement) appears, prefer the surface subcategory — its priors are far sharper than these.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

features_not_measurable_here.band_limit_high_hz: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.beats_per_minute: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.clipping_density: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.dc_offset: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.length_seconds: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.lufs: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.root_mean_square_level: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.sample_rate: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.stereo_width: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.total_harmonic_distortion: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json
features_not_measurable_here.voicing_ratio: left reasoned — see not_calibrated in fsd50k_calibration.json

measured_evidence.caveat: Amateur Freesound audio, mono, arbitrary normalization. Treat as a prior on the prior, not as truth. Duration, level, stereo width and band limit are NOT measurable here and are left reasoned.

explanation: Rain on a glass roof like a sunroof
or rain blown against a window as well.

discriminators: Second-brightest RAIN surface. Glass is stiff and lossy-in-the-bass: droplets excite a thin, glassy tick with a short ring — bright (centroid ~5.2 kHz) but with a decay_time_seconds_60db (~55 ms) well below RAIN-METAL's ~90 ms and almost no low_band_energy panel body.
Against RAIN-METAL: metal rings roughly twice as long and reaches higher; glass is brighter than everything else but deader than metal.
Against RAIN-PLASTIC: plastic and vinyl are lossy polymers — much duller (centroid ~3.2 kHz) and with a boomier mid/low body.
A window pane recorded from indoors is RAIN-INTERIOR (low-passed by the structure), not RAIN-GLASS.

Defined by the RECORDING POSITION, not the surface: rain heard through a wall, roof or windshield. The building is a low-pass filter, so this is the only RAIN subcategory with a collapsed band_limit_high_hz (~7 kHz) and dominant low_band_energy.

That LP signature is a real, measurable cue — but it is a transmission-path cue, and an outdoor cloth or vegetation recording can mimic the darkness. Damping alone will not settle it; the words 'interior', 'inside' or 'indoor' should.

Against RAIN-CLOTH and RAIN-VEGETATION: those are dark because the SURFACE absorbs the high frequencies at the impact; INTERIOR is dark because the WALL removes them in transit. band_limit_high_hz (a hard shelf) separates the two mechanisms from a gradual spectral_slope_db_per_octave roll-off.
Narrower stereo_width and a more stationary bed than any exterior RAIN recording.

explanation: so exterior rain on a car

discriminators: The BRIGHT, RINGING extreme of the RAIN material-impedance axis. A metal roof or car panel is a low-loss plate: each droplet excites flexural modes that RING. Highest spectral_centroid_hz (~6 kHz), flattest spectral_slope_db_per_octave (-3.5 dB/oct), and by far the longest decay_time_seconds_60db (~90 ms) of any RAIN surface — an order of magnitude above RAIN-VEGETATION.
The ring is inharmonic and non-pitched (a plate, not a bell): high inharmonicity with low harmonicity. That is what distinguishes a metal 'ping' from a tonal impact.
Against RAIN-GLASS: glass is nearly as bright but stiffer and thinner-sounding, with a much shorter ring (~60 ms vs ~90 ms) and less low-frequency panel body.
Against RAIN-CONCRETE: concrete is a rigid half-space, so it SLAPS and does not ring at all (decay ~20 ms). Metal ringing versus concrete slapping is the cleanest pair on this axis.
Subtle but physically real: this is a damping/brightness measurement, not a guess. Confirm with the filename before committing.

explanation: Rain on plastic or vinyl surfaces

discriminators: Plastic and vinyl are lossy polymer membranes: they thump and rattle rather than ring. Mid-dominant (mid_band_energy is the largest band), moderately dark (centroid ~3.2 kHz), with a short, damped decay (~30 ms) — well under RAIN-GLASS or RAIN-METAL.
A stretched plastic cover behaves as a drum head, so it carries more low/mid body (low_band_energy ~0.18) than any of the hard surfaces.
Against RAIN-METAL: metal is far brighter and rings 3x longer.
Against RAIN-CLOTH: cloth is even more damped and much darker; plastic still has an audible tick, cloth has almost none.
The plastic/wood boundary is the weakest pair on this axis: both are lossy plates in the same mid-dark range. Expect to need the filename.

explanation: leaves and grass. Jungle or tropical rain on plant leaves.

discriminators: UCS: 'rain on plants, leaves and grass'. Foliage is a diffuse, high-loss scatterer: thousands of tiny lightweight leaves each absorb the droplet. The result is the DEADEST decay_time_seconds_60db in RAIN (~12 ms), a steep spectral_slope_db_per_octave (-9 dB/oct), and a dark centroid (~2 kHz).

Diffuse means no individual drop is resolvable: the highest onset_rate_per_second and stationarity, the highest spectral_flatness, and the LOWEST crest_factor of any RAIN surface. It reads as a soft hiss, not a patter.
Against RAIN-METAL: the two poles of the whole axis. Metal is bright, spiky and ringing; vegetation is dark, smooth and dead.

Against RAIN-CLOTH: cloth is comparably damped but darker still, and an umbrella or awning is a taut membrane, so individual drops DO produce a resolvable thump (higher crest_factor) — foliage does not.
Against RAIN-WOOD: wood is brighter and has a resolvable per-drop knock.

Rain on standing water: each droplet entrains a bubble whose Minnaert resonance gives a faintly PITCHED plop. Elevated harmonicity (~0.25) with low inharmonicity is unique among the RAIN surfaces — every other surface here is a purely inharmonic solid.
Water absorbs, so the surface is dark (centroid ~2.3 kHz) and dead (decay ~22 ms), with more low_band_energy than the hard surfaces from the cavity plops.
Against RAIN-CONCRETE: both are hard-surfaced in the sense of being non-resonant, but concrete slaps bright and inharmonic; puddles plop dark and pitched.
Against WATER-DRIP: same droplet physics, but RAIN-WATER is a dense texture (onset_rate_per_second ~50), not sparse isolated transients.

Wood is the classic lossy plate of the material-impedance axis: high internal damping kills the ring (decay ~35 ms) and rolls off the top (centroid ~2.6 kHz, slope -8 dB/oct), leaving a warm mid-band-dominant knock. Deck, barn or shed.
Against RAIN-METAL: the canonical impedance contrast. Metal is ~2.3x brighter and rings ~2.5x longer; wood is dark and dead.
Against RAIN-CONCRETE: similar deadness but concrete is markedly brighter and slappier; wood has the mid body of a plate, concrete the crack of a half-space.
Against RAIN-VEGETATION: leaves are darker still, far more diffuse, and have almost no resolvable per-drop transient.
